slides.body: It tells them you exist. That's all. ↓
Here's what a supervisor actually needs to move your track forward:

✓ High-quality WAV or AIFF
Not a stream. Not a download link. A file, ready immediately.

✓ Proper file naming
Format: ArtistName_TrackTitle_BPM_Key.wav
"finalfinalv3_mixdown.wav" doesn't cut it.

✓ Embedded metadata
Title, composer, ISRC, mood, genre, BPM — inside the file, not in an email. ✓ Rights status confirmed
One-stop or clearly defined split, documented before you pitch. ✓ Stems available
Not now. But ready when they ask. The work, in numbers. SoundCloud tells a supervisor:
→ A track exists
→ You have a profile That's not enough to move a placement forward. → Why streaming links fail supervisors every time
→ The file format issue — and what broadcast spec actually means
→ What proper metadata looks like inside a file
→ How to handle the rights conversation before it becomes a problem
→ What a professional submission package looks like Save this before your next outreach. #SyncReady #AfricanComposers #SyncMaster #SyncLicensing #MusicBusiness
